source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the figure, the transmitter \( T_X \) sends a wideband modulated RF signal via a coaxial cable to the receiver \( R_X \). The output impedance \( Z_T \) of \( T_X \), the characteristic impedance \( Z_0 \) of the cable, and the input impedance \( Z_R \) of \( R_X \) are all real.
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: Signal distortion implies impedance mismatch at both ends: \( Z_T \neq Z_0 \) and \( Z_R \neq Z_0 \).

choice_map.A: The signal gets distorted if \( Z_R \neq Z_0 \), irrespective of the value of \( Z_T \).
choice_map.B: The signal gets distorted if \( Z_T \neq Z_0 \), irrespective of the value of \( Z_R \).
choice_map.C: Signal distortion implies impedance mismatch at both ends: \( Z_T \neq Z_0 \) and \( Z_R \neq Z_0 \).
choice_map.D: Impedance mismatches do NOT result in signal distortion but reduce power transfer efficiency.

extraction_notes: image field is a PIL image object placeholder, not a path or URL
raw_answer_text mapped from solution letter C to corresponding choice text

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'In the figure', indicating the depicted setup is part of the prompt.
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false both point to nontrivial image reliance.
asset_registry_record shows an existing image and registry_passed=true.
The text already states the key entities and impedances, so the image may support context more than provide unique numerical detail.
multi_step: The problem involves reasoning over three impedance quantities: Z_T, Z_0, and Z_R.
The choices require comparing whether distortion follows from mismatch at one end, both ends, or neither, which is more than a single lookup.
There is no explicit numeric calculation or long derivation in the provided text, so the reasoning depth appears moderate rather than high.
verifiability: This is effectively a 4-choice question with explicit answer candidates.
answer_text matches the content of choice C exactly, enabling straightforward option-level checking.
answer_present=true, answer_type is short text/text, and registry_passed=true, which supports reliable downstream verification.
